contest.title: IAEPC Preliminary Contest (Codeforces Round 999, Div. 1 + Div. 2)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints; liars may make either true or false claims, but two liars may not be adjacent.
Official hints identify the no-adjacent-liars restriction as the key to reducing the quadratic counting DP; the tutorial body was lazy-loaded in the consulted page.
Confirms the one-dimensional recurrence over prefixes ending at an honest person: compare a_i with a_{i-1} or a_{i-2}+1.
Cross-checks the equivalent honest/liar two-state DP, initialization, and final sum of both ending states.
Re-derived the recurrence from exact liar-prefix counts and exhaustively matched it against all assignments and claim arrays through n=5.

reference_candidates.notes: Codeforces status confirms an accepted implementation; the source page was blocked by a browser challenge, so it was not used as evidence.

annotation.summary: Count honesty/liar assignments in a line, with no adjacent liars, such that every honest person's claimed number of liars to their left is exact.
annotation.constraints: Up to 1e4 test cases; 1 <= n <= 2e5, sum of n <= 2e5, and 0 <= a_i <= n. Counts are taken modulo 998244353, so a linear solution is required.
annotation.core_idea: Let f_i count valid prefixes in which person i is honest. Use an artificial honest position 0 with f_0=1 and a_0=0, and set f_1=[a_1=0]. If i and i-1 are both honest, their liar-prefix counts are equal, giving the transition [a_i=a_{i-1}]f_{i-1}. If i is honest and i-1 is a liar, then i-2 is honest and exactly that intervening liar is added, giving [a_i=a_{i-2}+1]f_{i-2}. Thus for i>=2, f_i=[a_i=a_{i-1}]f_{i-1}+[a_i=a_{i-2}+1]f_{i-2}. A configuration ending with liar i must have honest i-1 and imposes no condition on a_i, so the answer is f_n+f_{n-1}. This is equivalently a two-state DP H_i,L_i with L_i=H_{i-1}.
annotation.complexity: O(n) time and O(1) extra memory per test case with rolling states; O(sum n) time overall.
tricks: A liar's numerical claim is unconstrained even when it happens to be true.
No adjacent liars makes the previous honest claim at most two positions away.
Compare neighboring honest claims instead of storing the total liar count as a DP dimension.
Use an artificial honest position 0 to cover the case where person 1 is a liar and person 2 is honest.

solution_variants.name: linear-honest-endpoint-counting-dp
solution_variants.summary: Maintain f_i for prefixes ending with honest i, apply the two locally checkable transitions from honest i-1 or liar i-1, and add f_n+f_{n-1}. An equivalent implementation keeps explicit honest/liar ending states.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n) time and O(1) extra memory.

tags.evidence: The DP values count valid honesty/liar configurations modulo 998244353, and the final answer sums the two possible ending statuses.
After using the no-adjacent-liars rule, the prefix needs only the last honesty status, equivalently the two rolling counts H_i and L_i, with transitions decided by local comparisons of a_i.
